name: FB Post: Angeles Collapse - The Permit Enforcement Gap
markdown_description:
## Hook
A sari-sari store owner in Quezon City needs three months of paperwork to legally sell five-peso candies. A nine-story building in Angeles City got a 2025 work stoppage order, ignored it, added an unpermitted floor, and was still standing tall on May 23. On May 24 at 3 AM, that building collapsed.

## Core argument (2-3 lines)
The Angeles City building collapse (5 dead, 15 missing as of May 28) exposes a selective enforcement gap, not a permit gap. The same state that buries SME owners under months of architectural, structural, electrical, sanitary and fire-safety signatures failed to enforce a 2025 DOLE work stoppage order against a 9-story commercial project with unpermitted floor additions. The rules exist. The state has just decided who they apply to.

## Constitutional citation
Article II, Section 4: "The prime duty of the Government is to serve and protect the people."
